hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.ar:
803  -  وَّق َّال إ ِن ْزَّإهِي مُّ ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  مَّحْمُّوذٍ لِلزِّص َّا عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  إَّلز َّج ُّلُّ ي ُّصَّلِّى  عَّلَّى سَّزِي زٍ مِن ْ سَّاح ٍ وَّ ي َّسْح ُّذُّ عَّلَّى إَّلس َّاح ِ 
ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Dawud Al -Sarmi asked Abu Al -Hasan Imam Ali ibn Muhammad Al -Hadi {a.s} 
about going on a journey where there may be no place to pray except on snow. How 
should he pray in such a case?  
Imam {a.s} replied: "If you can avoid prostrating on the snow, then do not prostrate on 
it. But if it is not possible, then level it and prostrate on it."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 390 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 261 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, 
Part 2, p. 310 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 336 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 452 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1051 • Wasail Al-Shia, 
Vol. 5, p. 164 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 802
reference.chapter: GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.en:
Ibrahim ibn Abi Mahmoud said to Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha {a.s} about a man 
who prays on a bed made of teakwood and prostrates on the teakwood itself.  
Imam {a.s} replied: "Yes, there is no issue”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 261 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 310 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 
8, p. 744 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 178 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 364 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 803

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.ar:
805  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ سَّمَّاعَّه ُّ ن ْن ُّ مِهْزَّإن َّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ْ لُّحُّومِ إَّلسِّن َّاعِ مِن َّ إَّلط َّي ْزِ وَّ إَّلذ َّوَّإب ِّ ق َّال 
لَّحْمِهَّا ق َّا ِن  َّا ن َّكْزَّهُّهُّ وَّ إ َّم َّا إَّلْح ُّلُّوذُّ ق َّارْكَّن ُّوإ عَّلَّي ْهَّا وَّ لأَّ ي َّلْن َّسُّوإ مِن ْهَّا س َّي ْن اً ي ُّصَّل ُّون َّ ق ِي هِ . 
 وق ال إ ن ى  رص ى  إلله عن ه ق ى  رسالي ه إ لى : لأ ن ا س ن الصلاة  ق ى  س عز وون ز كل ما إ كلث  لحمه وإ ن  كان  علي ك  ع ي زة من  سن ح اب   
إ و سمور إ و ق ن ك  وإ رذب  إلصلاة  ق ان ز عه، ق ذ روى  ق ى  ذ لك  رج ض   وإ ي اك  إ ن  ي صلى  ق ى  ي علث  ولأ ق ى  إلي وب  إلذ ى  ي لي ه من   
ي حي ه وق وق ه.
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Imam Al -Baqir {a.s} that He said:  
"There is no issue with praying on mats made from reeds or on palm-leaf mats, and on 
any plant-based material, except for fruit”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 261 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 311 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 
8, p. 744 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 345 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 804

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.en:
Sama’a ibn Mehran asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} regarding 
eating the meat of wild animals, including birds and other beasts.  
Imam responded, "As for eating their meat, we dislike it, but as for their skins, you may 
use them to ride on but do not wear anything made from them for prayer."  
[AL SADUQ] 
My father, may Allah {SWT} be pleased with him, said in his letter to me:  
"There is no issue with praying in clothing made from the hair or fur of any animal 
whose meat is permissible to eat. However, if you have garments of squirrel, sable, or 
marten fur, remove them before prayer, though concessions have been narrated for 
this.  
Be cautious not to pray wearing fox skins or clothing directly touching such skins."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 805

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.ar:
807  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ مَّهْز ِي َّارَّ ق َّالَّ: رَّإ َّي ْث ُّ إ َّن َّا ج َّعْق َّزٍ إَّلي  َّان ِى َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم ي ُّصَّلِّى  إَّلْق َّزِي ص َّه َّ وَّ ع َّي ْزَّهَّا ق ِى  ج ُّن  َّه ِ ج َّز ٍّ 
طَّارُّون ِى ٍّ وَّ كَّسَّان ِى  ج ُّن  َّه َّ ج َّز ٍّ وَّ ذ َّكَّزَّ إ َّن  َّهُّ لَّن ِسَّهَّا عَّلَّى ن َّذَّن ِهِ وَّ صَّل َّى ق ِي هَّا وَّ إ َّمَّزَّن ِى  ن ِالص َّلاَّة ِ ق ِ ي هَّا.
hadiths.en:
It has been narrated from Sulayman ibn Ja'far al -Ja'fari that He said: "I saw 
Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha {a.s} praying in a silk cloak (jubbah made of khazz)." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 262 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 359 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 806

reference.location: Volume 1 › GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.ar:
808   -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ ي َّحْي َّى ن ْن ِ إ َّن ِى  عِمْزَّإن َّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ: كَّي َّن ْث ُّ إ ِلَّى إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ إَّلي  َّان ِى  عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق ِى إَّلسِّن ْح َّاب ِ وَّ إَّلْق َّن َّك ِ 
وَّ إَّلْح َّز ِّ وَّ ق ُّلْث ُّ ج ُّعِلْث ُّ ق ِذَّإك َّ إ ُّجِث  ُّ إ َّن ْ لأَّ ي ُّح ِي ن َّن ِى  ن ِالي  َّق ِي  َّه ِ ق ِى  ذ َّلِك َّ ق َّكَّي َّث َّ ن ِح َّطِّهِ إ ِلَّى .
hadiths.en:
Ali ibn Mahziyar narrated: "I saw Abu Ja'far al -Thani (Imam Muhammad ibn Ali 
Al-Jawad {a.s}) praying both the obligatory and non -obligatory prayers in a cloak made 
of fine silk fabric (khazz taruni). He gave me a cloak made of khazz, informed me that 
he had worn it on his body and prayed in it, and instructed me to pray in it as well."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 262 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 411 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 
359 • Awalim al-Ulum, Vol. 23, p. 387 • Awalim al-Ulum, Vol. 23, p. 521 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 807

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.ar:
809   -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ ذَّإوُّذَّ إَّلص َّزْمِى ِّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ: سَّا َّلَّ رَّج ُّل  إ َّن َّا إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ إَّلي  َّالِث َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ِ إَّلص لاَّة ِ ق ِى  إَّلْح َّز ِّ ي ُّع َّس  ُّ 
ن ِوَّن َّزِ إَّلْأ َّرَّإن ِث ِ ق َّكَّي َّث .   
وهذ ة رج صه  إلأ ج ذ  ن ها ما ج ور ورإذها ما ي وم وإلأ صل ما ذ كزة إ ن ى    رجمه  إللهق ى   رسالي ه إ لى : وصل ق ى  إلح ز  ما لم ي كن   
مع س وس ا ن ون ز إلأ رإن ث ، وق ال ق ي ها: ولأ ي صل ق ى  ذي ن اح  ولأ جزي ز ولأ وس ى  ولأ ق ى  س ى  من  إ ن زي سم محض  إ لأ إ ي كون  ي ون ا 
سذإة إ ن زي سم ولحمي ه ق طن  إ و كي ان .
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Yahya ibn Abi Imran that He said: ‘I wrote to Abu Ja'far al-
Thani (Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Jawad {a.s}) concerning clothing made from squirrel 
(sanjab), mink (fanak), and silk (khazz), and I said: 'May I be your ransom, I would like 
you not to respond with dissimulation (taqiyya) regarding this matter.'  
The Imam {a.s} wrote back in his own handwriting, 'Pray in them.'" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 262 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 411 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 
349 • Awalim al-Ulum, Vol. 23, p. 337 • Awalim al-Ulum, Vol. 23, p. 386 • Awalim al-Ulum, Vol. 23, p. 521 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 808

reference.location: Volume 1 › GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.ar:
810  -  وَّ كَّي َّث َّ إ ِن ْزَّإهِي مُّ ن ْن ُّ مَّهْز ِي َّارَّ إ ِلَّى إ َّن ِى  مُّحَّم َّذٍ إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  ي َّسْا َّلُّهُّ عَّن ِ إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ ق ِى  إَّلْق ِزْمِز ِ ق َّا ِن  َّ إ َّصْحَّان َّن َّا 
ي َّي َّوَّق  َّوْن َّ عَّن ِ إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ ق ِي هِ ق َّكَّي َّث .    
ق ال مصن ف  هذ إ إلكي اب    رجمه إلله: وذ لك  إ ذ إ لم ي كن  إلق زمز  من  إ ن زي سم محض  وإلذ ى  ن هى عن ه هو ما كان  من  إ ن زي سم محض .
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Dawud al-Sarmi that a man asked Abu al-Hasan al-Thalith 
(Imam Ali ibn Muhammad Al -Hadi {a.s}), about praying in silk (khazz) that is mixed with 
rabbit fur.  
The Imam wrote in response: "That is permissible."  
[AL SADUQ] 
This is a leniency for which the one who acts upon it will be rewarded, and the one who 
rejects it will be blameworthy. The original ruling as mentioned by (the narrator's) 
father in his letter states: "Pray in silk (khazz) as long as it is not mixed with rabbit fur." 
He also stated: "Do not pray in brocade, pure silk, or embroidered silk, except if it is a 
garment with silk warp and cotton or linen weft." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 262 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 213 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 809
reference.chapter: GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.location: Volume 1 › GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٩، ح ٨٠٩

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.ar:
811 - وَّ كَّي َّث َّ إ ِلَّي ْهِ: ق ِى  إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّح ْعَّلُّ ق ِى ج ُّن  َّي ِهِ ن َّذَّلَّ إَّلْق ُّطْن ِ ق َّز  إً هَّلْ ي ُّصَّلِّى  ق ِي هِ ق َّكَّي َّث ."   
ي عن ى  ن ه ق ز  إلمعز  لأ ق ز  إلأ ن زي سم.    
وق ذ ورذب  إلأج ن ار ن الن هى  عن  لن س إلذي ن اح  وإلحزي ز وإلأ ن زي سم إلمحض  و إلصلاة  ق ي ه للزج ال، وورذب  إلزج صه  ق ى  لن س 
ذ لك  للن ساء ولم ي زذ ن ح وإر  صلاي هن  ق ي ه ق الن هى  عن  إلصلاة  ق ى  إلأ ن زي سم إلمحض  على إلعموم للزج ال وإلن ساء   جي ى ي ح صهن  
ج ن ز ن الأطلاق  لهن  ق ى  إلصلاة  ق ي ه كما ج صهن  ن لن سه ولم ي طلق  للزج ال لن س إلحزي ز وإلذي ن اح  إ لأ ق ى  إلحزب ، ولأ ن ا س ن ه  
وإ ن  كان  ق ي ه ي ماي ي ل. روى ذ لك  سماعه  ن ن  مهزإن  عن  إ ن ى  عن ذ إلله علي ه إلسلام .
hadiths.en:
Ibrahim ibn Mahziyar wrote to Abu Muhammad Imam Hasan ibn Ali {a.s}, asking 
about praying in garments dyed with crimson (qirmaz), saying, "Our companions avoid 
praying in it."  
The Imam wrote back: "There is no problem with it at all, and all praise is due to Allah 
{SWT}." 
[AL SADUQ] 
The author of this book commented:  
This ruling applies if the crimson-dyed garment is not made of pure silk. The prohibition 
pertains only to garments made from pure silk. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 810
reference.chapter: GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.location: Volume 1 › GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٩، ح ٨١٠

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.en:
He (Ibrahim ibn Mahziyar) wrote to Imam Abu Muhammad al-Hasan {a.s} asking 
about a man who replaces cotton in his cloak with qazz (a type of silk): "Can he pray in 
it?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "Yes, there is no problem with it."  
[AL SADUQ] 
The explanation is that it refers to qazz from goats, not pure silk qazz.  
The narrations have prohibited wearing brocade (dibaj), silk, and pure silk (ibrisim) and 
praying in them for men.  
However, there is an allowance for women to wear such materials, though it does not 
extend to praying in them. The prohibition on praying in pure silk applies generally to 
both men and women, unless a specific exception has been made for women 
regarding prayer as there has been for wearing it. For men, wearing silk and brocade is 
only permitted in times of war. It is also permissible even if it contains images.  
This was narrated by Sama’a ibn Mehran from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 811
reference.chapter: GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.location: Volume 1 › GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٩، ح ٨١١

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.en:
Yusuf ibn Muhammad ibn Ibrahim narrated from the Imam {a.s} that He said:  
"There is no issue if a garment's warp, buttons, or trim is made of silk; what is disliked 
for men is pure silk (mubham)”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 264 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 208 • Al -Istibsar, 
Part 1, p. 386 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 20, p. 726 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 375 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 80, p. 240 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 812
reference.chapter: GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.location: Volume 1 › GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٩، ح ٨١٢

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.ar:
814 - وَّ سَّا َّلَّ مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ إ ِسْمَّاعِي لَّ ن ْن ِ ن َّز ِي غٍ إ َّن َّا إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ إَّلزِّص َّا عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ِ إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ ق ِى  إَّلي  َّوْب ِ إَّلْمُّعْلَّمِ ق َّكَّزِةَّ مَّا 
ق ِي هِ مِن َّ إَّلي  َّمَّاي ِي لِ .    
ولأ ي ح ور  إلصلاة  ق ى  ي كه  رإ سها من  إ ن زي سم، ولأ ن ا س ن الصلاة  ق ى  إلق زإء إلح وإرر مي ه  وما ي ذن غ  ن ا رض  إلحح ار  ، ولأ ن ا س 
ن الصلاة  ق ى  صوف  إلمي ي ه  لأن  إلصوف  لي س ق ي ه روح.
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Mus'ab ibn Abdul Malik al -Basri that He said:  
"There is no issue in taking (a piece) from the silk covering of the Ka’ba and using it as 
a cover for a Quran or making it into a prayer mat to pray upon."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 264 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 734 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 
378 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 13, p. 258 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 813
reference.chapter: GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.location: Volume 1 › GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٩، ح ٨١٣

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.ar:
815  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ سَّمَّاعَّه ُّ ن ْن ُّ مِهْزَّإن َّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ْ ي َّق ْلِي ذِ إَّلس َّي ْف ِ ق ِى  إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ ق ِي هِ إَّلْع ِزَّإءُّ وَّ إَّلْكَّي ْمُّح ْث ُّ  
ق َّق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Ismail ibn Bazi' asked Abu Al -Hasan Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar -
Ridha {a.s}, about praying in a garment with patterns (decorations), and the Imam 
disliked it if it contained images (representations). 
[AL SADUQ] 
Praying in a belt (headband) with a silk end is not permitted. There is no issue with 
praying in garments made from Khwarazmian furs or those tanned in the land of Hijaz. 
Also, there is no issue with praying in the wool of a dead animal because wool does 
not have a soul. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 264 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 437 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 814
reference.chapter: GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.location: Volume 1 › GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٩، ح ٨١٤

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.ar:
816   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ إَّلز َّي  َّان ِ ن ْن ِ إَّلص َّلْث ِ إ َّن َّا إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ إَّلي  َّالِث َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :   عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّا ْج ُّذ ُّ مِن ْ س َّعْزِةِ وَّ إ َّط ْق َّارِةِ ي ُّم َّ 
ي َّق ُّومُّ إ ِلَّى إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ مِن ْ ع َّي ْزِ إ َّن ْ ي َّن ْق ُّص َّهُّ مِن ْ ي َّوْن ِهِ ق َّق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Sama’a ibn Mehran asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about 
wearing a sword during prayer that has glue or leather (adhesive substances, such as 
glue made from animal parts) on it.  
Imam {a.s} replied: "There is no problem as long as you do not know that it is from a dead 
(non-slaughtered) animal”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 265 • Al-Fusul al-Muhimmah, Part 2, p. 56 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 815
reference.chapter: GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.location: Volume 1 › GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٩، ح ٨١٥

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.ar:
817 - وَّ سَّا َّلَّ ي ُّون ُّسُّ ن ْن ُّ ي َّعْق ُّوب َّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم : عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي ُّصَّلِّى  وَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلْن ُّزْطُّلَّه ُّ ق َّق َّال .   
وسمعث  مس ان ح ن ا رص ى  إلله عن هم ي ق ولون : لأ ي ح ور  إلصلاة  ق ى  إلطان ق ي ه   ولأ ي ح ور  للمعي م إ ن  ي صلى  إ لأ وهو مي حن ك .
hadiths.en:
Ali ibn al -Rayan ibn al -Salt asked Imam Abu al -Hasan al -Thalith {a.s} about a 
man who cuts his hair or trims his nails and then goes to pray without shaking the 
remnants off his clothes.  
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no issue (no problem)." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 265 • The Makarem al -Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 66 • Bihar Al-
Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 123 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 816
reference.chapter: GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.location: Volume 1 › GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٩، ح ٨١٦

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.ar:
818   -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّم َّار  إَّلس َّان َّاطِى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال -  «
جَّن َّكِهِ ق َّا َّصَّان َّهُّ إ َّلَّم  لأَّ ذَّوَّإءَّ لَّهُّ ق َّلاَّ ي َّلُّومَّن  َّ إ ِلأ َّ ن َّق ْسَّه .
hadiths.en:
Yunus ibn Ya'qub asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about a man 
praying while wearing a burṭulah (a type of hat or head covering).  
The Imam said: "It does not harm him (there is no issue)."  
[AL SADUQ] 
And I heard our esteemed scholars say that it is not permissible to pray in a ṭabaqiyyah 
(a specific type of head covering), and a person wearing a turban (mu ʿtamim) must 
have it wrapped (with a part of the turban under the chin).  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 817
reference.chapter: GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.location: Volume 1 › GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٩، ح ٨١٧

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.en:
Ammar al-Sabati narrated from Imam Al-Sadiq {a.s} that He said: "Whoever goes 
out on a journey without wrapping the turban under his chin and suffers from an 
ailment for which there is no remedy, let him blame none but himself”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 266 • Makarem al-Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 245 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 
12, p. 398 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 20, p. 745 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 402 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 818
reference.chapter: GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.location: Volume 1 › GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٩، ح ٨١٨

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.ar:
820  -  وَّق َّال عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم ن ِّى   
لَّأ َّعْح َّث ُّ مِم َّن ْ ي َّا ْج ُّذ ُّ ق ِى  جَّاج َّه ٍ وَّ هُّوَّ مُّعْي َّم   ي َّحْث َّ جَّن َّكِهِ كَّي ْف َّ لأَّ ي ُّق ْص َّى جَّاج َّي ُّهُّ .
hadiths.en:
The truthful one, Imam Al-Sadiq {a.s}, said: "I guarantee that whoever leaves his 
house with his turban wrapped under his chin will return to it safely."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 819
reference.chapter: GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.location: Volume 1 › GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٩، ح ٨١٩

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.ar:
821 - وَّق َّال إَّلن  َّن ِى  ُّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ : « . 
وذ لك  ق ى  إ ول إلأسلام وإن ي ذإن ه
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}, said:  
"I am amazed by one who goes about seeking to fulfil a need while in a state of ritual 
purity (wudu)  -  how is it that his need is not met?  
And I am amazed by one who seeks to fulfil a need while wearing his turban wrapped 
under his chin  -  how is it that his need is not met?" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 266 • Meftah al-Falah, Part 1, p. 165 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 820
reference.chapter: GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.location: Volume 1 › GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٩، ح ٨٢٠

hadiths.chapter: 39. GARMENTS PERMISSIBLE FOR PRAYER AND WHAT
hadiths.ar: 822 - وَّ ق َّذْ ن َّق َّلَّ عَّن ْهُّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ إ َّهْلُّ إَّلْح ِلاَّف ِ إ َّي ْص اً: إ َّن  َّهُّ إ َّمَّزَّ ن ِالي  َّلَّحِّى  وَّ ن َّهَّى عَّن ِ إَّلأِق ْي ِعَّاطِ .
hadiths.en:
The Prophet, said: "The distinction between the Muslims and the polytheists 
is the wearing of turbans." 
[AL SADUQ] 
This was during the early period of Islam and its establishment.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 266 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 20, p. 745 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, 
p. 403 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 39, H. 821
